title: Field Pit-Wave Response Features
summary: Pit stops often cascade: once rivals stop, nearby cars may respond to defend against an undercut or avoid losing track position.
rationale: Pit stops often cascade: once rivals stop, nearby cars may respond to defend against an undercut or avoid losing track position. The current feature set has individual pit counts but little field-level pit-wave pressure.
implementation_hint: Within Race_Year and LapNumber, compute current/prior-lap PitStop counts and rates overall, by compound, by position band, and for nearby positions such as +/-2. Add lag1/lag2 pit-wave intensity, cumulative stops this window, and whether cars ahead or behind have just pitted.
expected_effect: Should catch synchronized strategy windows and undercut-response laps that static tyre-life features miss.
risk: Same-lap field aggregates may be a timing assumption; validate both same-lap and lag-only variants under grouped race validation.
